Editors

Video editor portfolio examples

A strong portfolio is not one giant reel. It is a small set of matched examples that prove you can handle the exact editing workflow a team needs.

Match examples to the role

For YouTube work, show pacing, story, retention, and sponsor integration. For short-form social, show hooks, captions, rhythm, and versioning. For assistant editor roles, show organization, sync, proxies, and clean handoff habits.

Add notes that reduce guessing

Beside each example, explain your role, the source footage, turnaround time, software, revision load, and what you owned. Hiring teams need to know whether you cut, colored, mixed, captioned, organized, or only polished.

Keep a fast-send version

Create a short profile link or document with your top formats, two or three relevant examples, rate range, availability, software, timezone, and contact details. Referrals work better when your fit is easy to forward.

Want better editing work?
